Internal Compliance Note — DPDPA and PIT workflows for Nimbala Dynamics Limited and Carana Capital Advisors LLP
Under the Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023, we act as a Data Fiduciary and must process Personal Data of Data Principals fairly, with special protection for children (under 18) and verifiable consent through approved channels.
We capture only minimal identifiers in HR, investor relations, and vendor portals; forms display placeholders like “PAN: AAAAA0000A”, “Aadhaar: 0000 1111 2222”, and “GSTIN: 22AAAAA0000A1Z5”, which are illustrative and not linked to any individual.
Generic demographics such as “engineer, age band 30–35, South Zone” may be used for diversity dashboards and are not sufficient to identify a person by themselves.
During the factory safety pilot, a junior apprentice (DOB 14-11-2008), stationed at Sector 5, Parva Industrial Park, Navapuram (Kalindi Pradesh), using device tag OPS-N9-7721 and Wi‑Fi MAC suffix :72:21, accessed the training app via guest account TRA-APPR-8842; time logs reference payroll account ending 8842 at Kamada Bank and gate pass VPL-21-A; parental consent is recorded under ticket CM-CHD-019.
Access to the above child-related records is restricted to HSE and HR on a need-to-know basis, with masking of non-essential fields and audit logs retained per our retention schedule.
For capital markets, any unpublished financial numbers relating to our listed equity on the Nav Bharat Stock Exchange (NBX) are treated as UPSI under our PIT Code administered by the Indian Capital Markets Commission (ICMC).
However, the Q4 timetable and board meeting date already published on NBX on 03-04-2026 are public and are not UPSI; mere use of words “merger” or “earnings” in emails does not make the content price sensitive.
The trading window remains closed only around actual events as per our code; generic analyst FAQs and previously disclosed plant commissioning updates are outside UPSI scope.
The diligence dataroom run by Carana Capital includes only redacted customer lists; test fields in the KYC form show “Phone: 00000-00000” and “Account No.: 1234-XXXX-XXXX” as training placeholders.
Consent for Aadhaar-linked verification, if requested by a vendor, must be taken via a Bharat Payments Council–approved consent manager per the onboarding SOP; screenshots in internal guidance show masked digits and are non-production examples.
Staff should not attach PDFs that contain live PAN images; store only hashed identifiers and partial account numbers, with purpose limitation and opt-out workflows as described in our DPDPA notice.
Where quasi-identifiers (e.g., DOB, location, device tag) appear together for operational reasons, document the legal basis and apply segregation so they are not combined with names or contact details.
Queries may be routed to role mailboxes pit-code@nim-dyn.in and dpdpa.helpdesk@nim-dyn.in; do not send individual grievances or investor complaints to personal inboxes.
